texto: He who having lost one ideal refuses to give his heart and soul to another and nobler is like a man who declines to build a house on rock because the wind and rain ruined his house on the sand.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Constance Naden: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “He who having lost one ideal refuses to give his heart and soul to another and nobler is like a m…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, events, house pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e reflexao.
